_comment: Team convention — not an official Claude Code format. This manifest tracks published capabilities.

capabilities.name: software-architect
capabilities.description: Analyzes and designs system architecture; writes ADRs; reasons about trade-offs across security, performance, scalability, cost, and maintainability.

capabilities.name: functional-analyst
capabilities.description: Extracts and documents functional requirements, use cases, business processes, and acceptance criteria.

capabilities.name: functional-reconstruction
capabilities.description: Use when reconstructing the functional behaviour of an existing codebase. Produces feature lists, user flows, business rules, use cases, and functional dependencies in docs/functional/. Use before a migration, refactoring, or to document existing functionality for a new team.
capabilities.changelog: Initial release — skill variant extracted from catalog

capabilities.name: developer-java-spring
capabilities.description: Writes production-ready Java/Spring Boot code with clean architecture, JUnit 5 testing, Spring Security, structured logging, and RFC 7807 error handling.
capabilities.changelog: v1.1.0: Extracted standards to java-spring-standards, testing-standards, rest-api-standards skills

capabilities.name: technical-analyst
capabilities.description: Produces structured technical analysis: tech debt inventory, security posture, dependency vulnerabilities, observability gaps, and CI/CD assessment.

capabilities.name: developer-python
capabilities.description: Writes production-ready Python following PEP 8, type hints, pytest, pydantic v2, structlog, and uv dependency management. FastAPI and general backend.

capabilities.name: code-reviewer
capabilities.description: Reviews code for correctness, security, test coverage, performance, and maintainability. Produces structured review with blocking issues, suggestions, and overall recommendation.
capabilities.changelog: v0.2.0: Added skill-based language-specific review (fulfills v1.0 TODO)

capabilities.name: test-writer
capabilities.description: Writes unit, integration, and controller tests for existing code. Supports JUnit 5 + Mockito, pytest, and Jest. Identifies and fills test coverage gaps.
capabilities.changelog: v0.2.0: Delegates to testing-standards and java-spring-standards skills

capabilities.name: debugger
capabilities.description: Diagnoses bugs by tracing from symptom to root cause. Produces a debug report with root cause analysis and minimal targeted fix.

capabilities.name: api-designer
capabilities.description: Designs and reviews REST API contracts. Produces OpenAPI 3.1 YAML and design rationale. Enforces REST maturity, consistent status codes, and RFC 7807 errors.

capabilities.name: documentation-writer
capabilities.description: Writes technical documentation: READMEs, runbooks, API guides, architecture overviews, and onboarding guides. Reads code before writing — never documents what should be, only what is.

capabilities.name: presentation-creator
capabilities.description: Creates Accenture-branded PowerPoint presentations (.pptx) from project docs, estimation files, or any source materials. Handles business and technical audiences. Covers problem, solution, architecture, dependencies, and timeline. Uses python-pptx with full Accenture brand compliance.
capabilities.changelog: v0.2.0: Delegates brand constants to accenture-branding skill

capabilities.name: document-creator
capabilities.description: Creates Accenture-branded technical documents (PDF via HTML/Chrome or Word .docx) from project docs and estimation files. Covers executive summary, problem, solution, architecture, component inventory, effort estimate, risks. Handles business and technical audiences.
capabilities.changelog: v0.2.0: Delegates brand constants to accenture-branding skill

capabilities.name: java-spring-standards
capabilities.description: Use to retrieve authoritative Java/Spring Boot standards: package structure, layering rules, testing patterns (JUnit 5 + Testcontainers), error handling (RFC 7807), logging, security, observability, and Maven conventions.
capabilities.changelog: Initial release — extracted from developer-java-spring and java-spring-conventions policy

capabilities.name: testing-standards
capabilities.description: Use to retrieve authoritative testing standards: principles, scenario taxonomy, method naming, Arrange-Act-Assert structure, and JUnit 5, pytest, Jest framework templates.
capabilities.changelog: Initial release — extracted from test-writer and developer-java-spring

capabilities.name: accenture-branding
capabilities.description: Use to retrieve Accenture brand standards: color palette, python-pptx constants, CSS template for PDF generation, typography rules, slide layout specifications.

capabilities.name: rest-api-standards
capabilities.description: Use to retrieve REST API design standards: resource modeling, HTTP methods, status codes, versioning, pagination, RFC 7807 error format, and OpenAPI 3.1 rules.

capabilities.name: developer-frontend
capabilities.description: Use when writing, reviewing, or refactoring frontend code. Supports Angular, React (+ Next.js, TanStack Start, TanStack Query, TanStack Router), Vue 3, Qwik, and Vanilla JS/TS. Detects the project framework first and invokes only the skills relevant to that stack — does not load Angular skills for a React project or vice versa. Produces production-ready, typed, accessible, tested frontend code following the conventions of the detected framework.

capabilities.name: tech-analyst
capabilities.description: Use when analysing a repository's technical structure. Produces module maps, dependency graphs, bounded contexts, data flows, integration points, and semantic indices. Use as the first step in an analysis, migration, or architectural-understanding pipeline.

capabilities.name: java-expert
capabilities.description: Use to load Java 17+ core standards: records, sealed classes, Optional, Stream API, Lombok patterns, concurrency, custom exceptions, logging conventions, and document generation (Apache POI, iText). Does not cover Spring Boot, JPA, or layered architecture.

capabilities.name: spring-architecture
capabilities.description: Use to load Spring layered-architecture standards: Controller/Service/Repository/Entity structure, DTO separation, Bean Validation, global exception handling, mapper pattern, structured logging, naming conventions, and module implementation order.

capabilities.name: spring-data-jpa
capabilities.description: Use to load JPA/Hibernate standards: entity design, relations, fetch strategies, N+1 solutions, transaction management, JPQL queries, second-level cache, bulk operations, automatic auditing, and performance optimisations.

capabilities.name: spring-expert
capabilities.description: Use to load Spring Boot 3.x standards: IoC/DI, auto-configuration, profiles, ConfigurationProperties, WebClient for external APIs, Spring Security 6 with JWT, and testing patterns (MockMvc, @WebMvcTest, @SpringBootTest).

capabilities.name: postgresql-expert
capabilities.description: Use to load PostgreSQL standards: relational design, data modelling, data types, indices, performance tuning, Flyway migrations, transaction management, data integrity, security, and monitoring. Works alongside spring-data-jpa; does not duplicate the ORM layer.

capabilities.name: backend-documentation
capabilities.description: Use when generating enterprise technical documentation for a Java/Spring Boot backend. Reads pre-existing analyses and source code to produce a backend-doc.tex covering architecture, API reference, data model, business logic, security, and error handling. Ready for pandoc conversion.

capabilities.name: doc-expert
capabilities.description: Use when producing technical or functional documentation for Python/Streamlit, Java/Spring Boot, or Angular projects. Covers docstrings, flow descriptions, domain glossary, and module guides. Output is business-oriented, not implementation-oriented. Saves output to docs/.

capabilities.name: documentation-orchestrator
capabilities.description: Use when generating enterprise technical documentation for a full-stack project. Interprets a Word template, coordinates backend-documentation and frontend-documentation skills, ensures cross-layer consistency (DTO names, API contracts), and produces backend-doc.tex and frontend-doc.tex ready for pandoc.

capabilities.name: frontend-documentation
capabilities.description: Use when generating enterprise technical documentation for an Angular frontend. Reads pre-existing analyses and Angular code to produce a frontend-doc.tex covering module architecture, smart/dumb components, NgRx store, routing, API services, design system, and performance. Ready for pandoc conversion.

capabilities.name: functional-document-generator
capabilities.description: Use to convert existing functional documentation into an enterprise LaTeX document deliverable to stakeholders. Reads from docs/functional/, interprets a provided Word template, and generates a complete .tex file ready for pandoc conversion to .docx. Does not invent functionality not supported by the source content.

capabilities.name: angular-expert
capabilities.description: Use to load Angular standards: component architecture, smart/dumb pattern, OnPush change detection, RxJS integration, Reactive Forms, lazy loading, and SOLID principles applied to Angular. Use for new components, FE refactoring, or Angular architectural review.

capabilities.name: ngrx-expert
capabilities.description: Use to load NgRx standards: store design, event-driven actions, pure reducers, memoised selectors, effects, facade pattern, state normalisation, and guidance on when NgRx is appropriate versus simpler alternatives.

capabilities.name: rxjs-expert
capabilities.description: Use to load RxJS standards for Angular: naming conventions, flattening strategies (switchMap/mergeMap/concatMap), subscription management, memory safety, stream combination, error handling, and pipeline clarity. Covers anti-patterns to eliminate.

capabilities.name: css-expert
capabilities.description: Use to load CSS/SCSS standards: design tokens, BEM naming, specificity rules, modularity, mobile-first responsive design, theming, and layout patterns. Use for SCSS refactoring, design system integration, or eliminating fragile inline styles.

capabilities.name: design-expert
capabilities.description: Use when designing layouts, mockups, or style specifications for new frontend components. Applies the company design system and coordinates with the project's framework skill and css-expert. Invoke before implementing any new UI component.

capabilities.name: qwik-expert
capabilities.description: Use to load Qwik and Qwik City standards: resumability, lazy components, signals, server-side loaders/actions, and file-based routing. Use for web apps with extreme performance requirements and minimum Time To Interactive.

capabilities.name: nextjs
capabilities.description: Use to load Next.js 14+ App Router standards: React Server Components, Server Actions, file-based routing, metadata API, multi-level caching, and deployment patterns. Does not cover the Pages Router.

capabilities.name: react-expert
capabilities.description: Use to load React 18+ standards: component architecture, hooks, TypeScript integration, performance optimisation (memo/useCallback/useMemo), Suspense, concurrent features, and testing with React Testing Library. For data fetching use tanstack-query; for routing use tanstack; for SSR use nextjs.

capabilities.name: tanstack-query
capabilities.description: Use to load TanStack Query v5 standards: useQuery, useMutation, useInfiniteQuery, QueryClient configuration, cache invalidation, optimistic updates, and prefetching. Replaces useEffect for server state management in React.

capabilities.name: tanstack-start
capabilities.description: Use to load TanStack Start standards for full-stack React: SSR, Server Functions, streaming, file-based routing, and end-to-end type safety. Use for new full-stack React apps that do not require the Next.js ecosystem.

capabilities.name: tanstack
capabilities.description: Use to load TanStack Router standards: type-safe file-based routing, route definitions, loaders, search params, lazy routes, and per-route error boundaries. Type-safe alternative to React Router.

capabilities.name: vanilla-expert
capabilities.description: Use to load Vanilla JS/TS standards: Web Components, ES Modules, modern DOM APIs, Custom Events, Intersection/MutationObserver, strict TypeScript, and Vite bundling. Use for independent widgets, reusable libraries, or projects where a framework is overkill.

capabilities.name: vue-expert
capabilities.description: Use to load Vue 3 Composition API standards: components, composables, Pinia state management, Vue Router 4, TypeScript patterns, performance optimisation, and testing with Vitest and Vue Test Utils. Does not cover Vue 2 or the Options API.

capabilities.name: backend-orchestrator
capabilities.description: Use for complex or multi-skill backend tasks. Coordinates java-expert, spring-expert, spring-data-jpa, spring-architecture, and postgresql-expert; interprets the request context and guarantees cross-layer consistency from Controller to DB.

capabilities.name: frontend-orchestrator
capabilities.description: Use for complex or multi-skill frontend tasks. Coordinates Angular, NgRx, RxJS, React, Vue, Qwik, CSS/SCSS, Design, and FE Refactoring skills; guarantees architectural and stylistic consistency across design, implementation, and state management.

capabilities.name: python-expert
capabilities.description: Use to load Python production standards: mandatory type hints, project structure, Pydantic v2, pytest, structlog, and dependency management (uv/pip-tools). Covers FastAPI services, CLIs, data pipelines, and scripts. Does not cover Streamlit — use streamlit-expert for that.

capabilities.name: streamlit-expert
capabilities.description: Use to load Streamlit standards: page structure, session_state management, caching, reusable components, and PostgreSQL/API integration. Use for developing or maintaining Streamlit web apps. For pure Python logic use python-expert.

capabilities.name: dependency-resolver
capabilities.description: Use when a dependency conflict blocks another skill: incompatible library versions, breaking changes after an update, absent documentation, inconsistent behaviour, transitive conflicts, or deprecated APIs. Support skill only — do not activate for simple version updates without conflicts.

capabilities.name: refactoring-expert
capabilities.description: Use when refactoring code in any language to improve internal structure without changing functional behaviour. Applies SOLID, DRY, KISS, YAGNI, Separation of Concerns, high cohesion/low coupling, testability, and readability.

capabilities.name: caveman-commit
capabilities.description: Use when generating commit messages. Produces a terse, intent-focused Conventional Commits subject line (max 72 chars) with a body only when the reasoning is non-obvious. No emojis, no AI credits. Outputs message text in a code block only — does not run git.

capabilities.name: caveman-review
capabilities.description: Use when reviewing code and producing PR comments. Each comment follows the format L<line>: <severity> <problem>. <fix>. No hedging, no code repetition, no motivational asides. Produces comments ready to paste into a PR.

capabilities.name: caveman
capabilities.description: Use to activate token-efficient communication mode (approx. 75% token reduction). Switches responses to terse, direct prose: no filler words, no hedging, no pleasantries. Preserves exact technical terminology and code unchanged.

capabilities.name: orchestrator
capabilities.description: Use when a task spans multiple domains, requires several specialists, or is ambiguous in scope. Dynamically discovers available agents, decomposes the request into independent subtasks, dispatches agents in parallel where possible, and synthesises their outputs into a single coherent result. Stack-agnostic.
capabilities.changelog: Promoted from skill to agent: dynamic discovery, real Agent dispatch, synthesis

capabilities.name: indexing-supervisor
capabilities.description: Single entrypoint for indexing a legacy Python codebase (with optional Streamlit) into a markdown knowledge base inside the repository. Decomposes indexing into phases, dispatches Sonnet sub-agents in parallel where independent, escalates to the user on ambiguity, and produces a final synthesis. Phase 1 only — indexing and understanding, not migration.
capabilities.changelog: Initial beta release: opus supervisor + 7 sonnet sub-agents, KB at .indexing-kb/

capabilities.name: codebase-mapper
capabilities.description: Sub-agent of indexing-supervisor. Produces structural inventory: directory tree, file counts, language statistics, top-level package map, entrypoints. No semantic analysis.

capabilities.name: dependency-analyzer
capabilities.description: Sub-agent of indexing-supervisor. Extracts external dependencies (pyproject.toml, requirements.txt, setup.py) and builds the internal module dependency graph from imports. Detects circular dependencies and standalone packages.

capabilities.name: streamlit-analyzer
capabilities.description: Sub-agent of indexing-supervisor. Analyzes Streamlit-specific concerns: pages, session_state, widgets, caching, navigation, custom components, and migration-relevant anti-patterns.

capabilities.name: module-documenter
capabilities.description: Sub-agent of indexing-supervisor. Documents one Python package end-to-end at the API level: purpose, public interface, key data structures, internal organization. One invocation per package, parallelizable.

capabilities.name: data-flow-analyst
capabilities.description: Sub-agent of indexing-supervisor. Identifies data crossings between application and outside world: database access, external API calls, file I/O, environment variables, configuration sources.

capabilities.name: business-logic-analyst
capabilities.description: Sub-agent of indexing-supervisor. Extracts business rules, validation logic, and domain concepts. Produces a domain-level view (glossary, rules, state machines) independent of file structure.

capabilities.name: synthesizer
capabilities.description: Final consolidation sub-agent of indexing-supervisor. Reads all prior phase outputs from the KB and produces system overview, bounded context hypothesis, complexity hotspot map, indexing report, and the index page.

capabilities.name: functional-analysis-supervisor
capabilities.description: Single entrypoint for Phase 1 (AS-IS Functional Analysis) of a refactoring/migration workflow. Reads the knowledge base at .indexing-kb/ produced by Phase 0 indexing and orchestrates Sonnet sub-agents in 3 waves to produce a complete functional understanding in docs/analysis/01-functional/. Stack-aware (Streamlit/generic), strictly AS-IS (no target-tech references), generic and reusable across migration scenarios.
capabilities.changelog: Initial beta release: opus supervisor + 5 sonnet sub-agents + opt-in challenger, output at docs/analysis/01-functional/

capabilities.name: actor-feature-mapper
capabilities.description: Sub-agent of functional-analysis-supervisor (Wave 1). Extracts actors, roles, personas, and the full feature map of an application AS-IS from the .indexing-kb/ knowledge base. Couples actor and feature analysis because who-can-do-what is one concept. Strictly AS-IS.

capabilities.name: ui-surface-analyst
capabilities.description: Sub-agent of functional-analysis-supervisor (Wave 1). Inventories the UI surface AS-IS: screens, navigation map, component tree. Strong Streamlit awareness — treats each page-script as a screen and widgets as first-class components. Strictly AS-IS.

capabilities.name: io-catalog-analyst
capabilities.description: Sub-agent of functional-analysis-supervisor (Wave 1). Inventories all functional inputs, outputs, and the transformation matrix of an application AS-IS. Functional perspective (user-perceived I/O), not infrastructure perspective. Strictly AS-IS.

capabilities.name: user-flow-analyst
capabilities.description: Sub-agent of functional-analysis-supervisor (Wave 2). Derives use cases, user flows, and Mermaid sequence diagrams from Wave 1 outputs. Streamlit-aware — handles reactive rerun-driven flows that have no explicit routing. Strictly AS-IS.

capabilities.name: implicit-logic-analyst
capabilities.description: Sub-agent of functional-analysis-supervisor (Wave 2). Extracts IMPLICIT business and validation logic embedded in widget parameters, conditional rendering, state-driven branches, callback chains, and magic numbers. Highest value in Streamlit codebases. Complements business-logic-analyst (which captures explicit rules). Strictly AS-IS.

capabilities.name: functional-analysis-challenger
capabilities.description: Optional adversarial sub-agent of functional-analysis-supervisor (Wave 3). Cross-validates the full set of Phase 1 outputs by looking for gaps, contradictions, unverified claims, and AS-IS violations. Does NOT rewrite — only flags findings. Default ON in Streamlit mode, opt-in otherwise.
